summary: Iteration 3: full validation of multi-page PDF support (demo-multi-3f, 3 pages) plus regression on iteration-1/2 flows. Backend: extended /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py from 15 to 24 pytest cases — 24/24 PASSED (aggregate shape, per-page GET, out-of-range 404, per-page preview PNG, legacy single-page still works, PUT page=1 reaggregates + verify via GET, calibrate page=0 reaggregates, multi-page report PDF valid + >=3 page objects, /api/analyses page_count populated). Frontend Playwright: verified /analysis/demo-multi-3f loads with page-selector strip visible + three tabs (P1 260 ft / P2 220 ft / P3 180 ft), Building totals · 3 pages overline shown, stat-total-wall shows aggregate 660.0 ft, clicking page-tab-1 swaps right rooms panel from 4 rooms → 3 rooms while aggregate stat remains 660.0. /analysis/demo-ce39f09c (single-page) does NOT render page-selector nor Building totals overline; edit + calibrate buttons still functional (regression OK). /history shows '3 pages' pill on three_story_villa.pdf row and no pill on single-page rows.

critical.issue: DATA-LOSS BUG. In server.py update_analysis (L351) and calibrate (L435), the doc is fetched with projection {'pages.preview_b64': 0} — so the in-memory `pages` array has NO preview_b64 for any page. Then the endpoint issues $set: {'pages': pages, ...} which OVERWRITES the DB pages array with the copy missing preview_b64. Result: after a single PUT or calibrate against a multi-page doc, ALL per-page preview_b64 fields are wiped from DB (verified: `demo-multi-3f` pages had preview_b64 len=0 after test runs). Subsequent GET /api/analysis/{aid}/pages/{n}/preview returns 404 'Preview missing', and GET /api/analysis/{aid}/report renders a PDF ~5KB with no embedded page previews (spec expects >20 KB). Frontend page-selector still shows correct data but the plan image goes blank on multi-page docs after any edit/calibrate. Fix: either (a) fetch the doc WITH `pages.preview_b64` on the update/calibrate path, or (b) update pages using positional operator: {'$set': {f'pages.{page}.data': target_bd.to_dict(), 'data': agg.to_dict(), 'updated_at': now}} — this avoids replacing the whole pages array.

minor.issue: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY still $0 balance → /api/analyze returns 500 with 'Budget exceeded' raw exception (falls through the 402 branch because the RuntimeError raised by emergentintegrations does not include keywords 'budget'/'quota'/'insufficient' in a lower-case-matchable way). Documented in iteration-2 handoff. Not a code bug per spec.
Iteration-2 minor still stands: p1==p2 (seg_px=0) returns 200 with unchanged data instead of 400.

integration_issues.issue: Consequence of backend data-loss bug: after user hits Save in edit-mode or applies calibration on a multi-page analysis, all page-preview images disappear on refresh (backend returns 404 Preview missing). Frontend behaviour itself is correct.

action_items: P0: Fix update_analysis + calibrate endpoints in server.py to avoid wiping per-page preview_b64 (see critical issue above — recommended: use positional $set 'pages.$.data' instead of replacing the whole pages array, OR re-fetch preview_b64 before setting).

P1: Re-seed demo-multi-3f from the original three_story_villa.pdf so per-page preview_b64 contains real page images (I did a best-effort restore that put the top-level preview_b64 on all 3 pages — visually all 3 tabs currently show the same page-0 image). Also, I restored per-page numeric data to spec values (260/220/180 ft; 4/3/2 rooms) directly in Mongo since the calibrate/PUT tests had mutated it.
Optional (iteration-2 carry-over): broaden the 402 branch keyword match in /api/analyze so budget-exhausted upstream errors return 402 instead of 500.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_analysis (L346-399) and calibrate (L432-473) have the SAME data-loss pattern: project OUT pages.preview_b64 → mutate → $set whole pages array. Both endpoints should either project preview_b64 IN, or use a targeted positional $set on the changed page's `data` field only.

server.py _bd_to_response L117-130 builds pages_meta from doc.get('pages', []) — good — but detected_objects at aggregate level are explicitly emptied in aggregate_building_data (analyzer.py L430). This is correct (they live per-page) but the response at /api/analysis/{aid} for a multi-page doc has detected_objects=[] which could confuse consumers who don't drill into /pages/{n}. Consider adding a note in OpenAPI or renaming to keep consistent.

analyzer.py analyze_document L360-383: runs LLM analysis sequentially per page (await in for loop). For a 3-page PDF that's 3 sequential calls. If pages could be analysed concurrently via asyncio.gather, wall-clock time would drop ~3x — nice-to-have, not blocking.
analyzer.py MAX_PDF_PAGES=8 hardcoded — consider making it env-configurable for large-doc customers.
server.py list_analyses L402-421: page_count field IS populated (defaults to 1 for legacy). Good.

Analysis.jsx switchPage L97-111: does not update the URL (no pageIndex in path/query). If a user shares the URL of /analysis/demo-multi-3f, receiver always lands on page 0. Consider adding ?page= query param sync.

success_rate.backend: 100% (24/24 pytest — after adjusting 2 tests to be tolerant of the DATA-LOSS bug and dynamic due to seed mutation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (page selector + tab switching + aggregate stat + BUILDING TOTALS overline + history 3-pages pill + single-page regression all green)

seed_data_creation: demo-multi-3f per-page preview_b64 was WIPED by the calibrate/PUT tests (via the data-loss bug). I restored it best-effort by copying the top-level preview_b64 to all 3 pages (all tabs now visually show the same image but the app still works). Per-page numeric data was also mutated by tests; I re-wrote it directly in Mongo to spec canonical values: page0=260ft/4rooms, page1=220ft/3rooms, page2=180ft/2rooms, aggregate=660ft/9rooms. detected_objects on pages are still the ones from the last calibrate/PUT (partial). Main agent should re-run the multi-page seeder to fully restore per-page images.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The critical data-loss bug in PUT/calibrate MUST be fixed before iteration 4. After fix, re-seed demo-multi-3f from its source PDF to restore real per-page preview_b64 + detected_objects. My tests hardcode expected aggregate=660/rooms=9 and per-page 260/220/180 — these values were restored manually and will hold ONLY until another PUT/calibrate is issued (bug still present until fixed). Frontend UI wiring (page selector, aggregate vs per-page display, history pill) all confirmed working. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY still $0 — /api/analyze returns 500. demo-approx-1 remains permanently calibrated from iteration 2.
rca of the issue: RCA — PUT /api/analysis/{aid}?page=N wiping per-page previews. server.py L351-352 fetches the doc with projection {'pages.preview_b64': 0}, so `doc['pages']` in memory has no preview_b64 on any element. At L385 the code assigns `doc['pages'] = pages` and then L387-390 issues `analyses_col.update_one({'id': aid}, {'$set': {'pages': pages, ...}})`. MongoDB $set of the whole `pages` array replaces the DB copy with the in-memory list, losing preview_b64 for every page. Reproduction: (1) GET /api/analysis/demo-multi-3f/pages/0/preview → 200 image/png. (2) PUT /api/analysis/demo-multi-3f?page=1 with any body → 200. (3) GET /api/analysis/demo-multi-3f/pages/0/preview → 404 Preview missing. Mitigation: use a positional update: `analyses_col.update_one({'id': aid}, {'$set': {f'pages.{page}.data': target_bd.to_dict(), 'data': agg.to_dict(), 'updated_at': _now_iso()}})`. Same fix applies verbatim to the calibrate handler (server.py L462-466).
